summary: open-agents-ai ships a postinstall daemon that fingerprints the host and beacons over HTTP
details: The package's package.json declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks (with curl invocations) and ships dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs, a Node script that loads child_process, http, fs, and os, calls os.userInfo() and execSync, runs ping, and issues http.request GETs. This is the classic install-time host-fingerprint-and-beacon shape: the script executes automatically on `npm install`, collects identifying information about the installer's machine (username, host details, command output) and sends it to a remote endpoint via HTTP. There is no legitimate purpose for an installed package to spawn a daemon at install time that pings hosts and issues outbound HTTP carrying system information. Installing this package will run attacker-controlled code on the installer's machine and leak host identifiers off-box.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
